subparts.text: Define Factor of safety Stress Concentration Factor. CO 1
Sketch examples where the bolts are subjected to Shear stress only Tensile and shear stresses. CO 2
Why taper is provided on the cotter of cotter joint? CO 2
Differentiate between Shaft and Axle. CO 3
What is a Self Locking screw? CO 3
What is Bearing Characteristic Number? CO 4

questions.question_text: Describe various theories of failure in machine design and their application. CO 2

A circular member is subjected to a direct tensile load of 20 kN and a shear load of 12 kN . the yield stress in tension is 25KN/ 𝑐𝑚2. Determine the diameter of the member according to maximum principal stress theory and maximum shear stress theory using a factor of safety 2. CO 2

A double -riveted double strap butt joint is used to connect two plates each of 12 mm thickness, by means of 16 mm diameter rivets having a pitch of 48 mm. The rivets and plates are made of steel. The permissible stresses in tension, shear and compression are 80, 60 and 120 𝑁/𝑚𝑚2 respectively. Determine the efficiency of the joint CO 2
Design a knuckle joint to transmit 150 kN. The design stresses may be taken as 75 MPa in tension, 60 MPa in shear and 150 MPa in compression. CO 2

A double threaded power screw, used for lifting loads, has a nominal diameter of 30 mm and a pitch of 6 mm. The coefficient of friction at the screw threads is 0.1. Neglecting collar friction, calculate CO 3 efficiency of the screw with square threads efficiency with ACME threads.

A steel shaft 35 mm in diameter and 1200 mm long held rigidly at one end has a hand wheel 50 cm diameter keyed to the other end. The modulus of rigidity of steel is 80 × 103𝑁/𝑚𝑚2. What load tangentially applied to the rim of the wheel produces a torsional shear stress of 60 𝑁/𝑚𝑚2. How many degrees will the handwheel twist, when this load is applied? CO 3

subparts.text: What are the desired properties of bearing material?
What is meant by Hydrodynamic Lubrication? How is the oil pressure created in these bearings? CO 4

questions.question_text: The following data is given for a pair of spur gears with 20℃ full depth involute teeth: Number of teeth on pinion = 24 Number of teeth on gear = 56 Speed of pinion = 1200 rpm Module = 3 mm Service factor = 1.5 Lewis form factor for 24 teeth is 0.337 Face width = 30 mm Both gears are made of steel with an ultimate tensile strength of 600 N/𝑚𝑚2 . Using the velocity factor to account for the dynamic load, Calculate: i. Beam strength ii. Velocity factor iii. Rated power that gear can transmit without bending failure, if the factor of safety is 1.5 CO 4
